name: GWS Gmail Integration
description: Gmail: Send, read, and manage email using the Google Workspace gws CLI. Covers messages, threads, drafts, labels, settings, history, and push notifications.

covenant_rules: Prerequisite: gws-shared must be loaded first for authentication and global flags.
Always confirm with the user before sending emails or performing destructive mailbox operations (delete, permanent delete).
Never output full email bodies containing PII without --sanitize flag screening.
Use gws schema gmail.<resource>.<method> to inspect required parameters before constructing API calls.

triad_templates.left_hemisphere: Evaluate the Gmail API method, required parameters, and security implications for: {query}. Verify the operation respects mailbox privacy and data sensitivity boundaries.
triad_templates.right_hemisphere: Design the exact gws gmail command with proper resource, method, --params, --json, and --format flags for: {query}. Include pagination for list operations.
triad_templates.main_hemisphere: Coordinate Gmail operations. Check auth, inspect method schema via gws schema, construct the command, confirm destructive actions, execute, and present formatted results: {query}.

steps.name: Inspect Gmail API Method Schema
steps.action: Use 'gws schema gmail.<resource>.<method>' to inspect required parameters, types, and defaults for the target Gmail API call. Resources: users (getProfile, stop, watch), drafts, history, labels, messages, settings, threads.

steps.name: Construct Gmail Command
steps.action: Build the full 'gws gmail <resource> <method>' command with appropriate --params, --json, --format, --page-all, --output flags based on the schema inspection results.

steps.name: Confirm Destructive Operations
steps.action: If the operation is a delete, update, or send operation, present the command to the user for confirmation. For read-only operations (get, list, getProfile), proceed automatically.

steps.name: Execute Gmail Command
steps.action: Execute the gws gmail command in the terminal with proper flags. Use --dry-run for destructive operations if available.

steps.name: Format and Present Email Results
steps.action: Parse the JSON or table output and present a clean summary. For message lists show sender, subject, date snippet. For message content show body with PII sensitivity note.

steps.name: Gmail Operation Completed
steps.action: Confirm the Gmail operation result to the user. Provide next-step suggestions such as replying to a message, creating a filter, or managing labels.
